cost_thresholds._comment: Tune per-workspace. Defaults err on the side of surfacing. Lower thresholds = more findings per run; raise them once the team has cleared baseline noise. Run-rate thresholds activate once the runtime exposes pipeline_event_rollups via adl_get_route_metrics.

idle_definition._comment: A route is 'idle' if last_event_at is null OR older than lookback_days. The analyzer also rolls up days_since_last_event so the recommender can rank severity. Routes legitimately bursty (cron-driven sources firing once a quarter) should be exempted via a per-route override stored in cost:audit:cache memory namespace.

sink_cost_table._comment: Cost-per-event estimates by sink type. The recommender uses these against events_30d (from adl_get_route_metrics) to project monthly run-rate. Rough order-of-magnitude defaults; operators should override with their actual contracted rates per provider.
sink_cost_table._units: USD per event delivered (rough order of magnitude, not invoice-accurate)

company_glossary.route: A configured pipeline path from a source to one or more sinks. Defined in pipeline_routes table.
company_glossary.sink: A delivery destination (database, lake, queue, webhook). Defined in environment_sinks table.
company_glossary.source: An ingest origin (CDC bridge, SaaS connector, webhook receiver, direct push). Defined in workspace_sources table.
company_glossary.fan-out: Number of sinks attached to a single route. Multiplies per-event cost.
company_glossary.lifetime_volume_bucket: Quartile-style bucket (none/low/med/high) the analyzer assigns each route based on events_processed_lifetime relative to the workspace distribution. Used in place of a per-window run-rate until the runtime exposes window-based event counts.
